title: Vendor v5p3x timing-delay property audit
trigger: Clock phase/delay guesses keep recurring in model output and prior triage, so close the vendor DTS/source evidence explicitly.
findings: Vendor SDMMC0 has only commented sunxi-dly-* override examples; no active delay override is set for sdc0.
Vendor SDMMC1 has an active sunxi-dly-208M override and execute_tuning_in_kernel, but SDMMC1 is the SDIO-style node, not the SD-card SDMMC0 proof target.
BSP v5p3x source initializes default delay tables for 400k, 26M, 52M, 104M, 208M, and DDR modes.
BSP v5p3x has sunxi_of_parse_clk_dly(host) commented out in the init path, so DTS delay overrides are not a required SDMMC0 path in this BSP slice.
The local diagnostic tree already includes delay/phase diagnostics and a prior forced A733 drive-delay preservation test; the IDMAC stall remained before descriptor word 0 was consumed.

public_search.notes: Search still did not reveal a public archive hit or answer for the manually sent A733 SDMMC0 descriptor-fetch question. Public hits were unrelated or repeated the known U-Boot A733 PIO support.

reason: Vendor SDMMC0 does not use an active timing-delay override, and clock phase affects card signaling after command/data timing rather than normal-IDMAC descriptor memory reachability. Prior delay diagnostics did not change the descriptor-fetch stall.
next: Do not retest clock phase or sunxi-dly properties as standalone descriptor-fetch fixes.
Keep waiting for a maintainer/list reply or a source-backed SDMMC0 firewall/security/master-permission bit.
